table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the the element can appear in the instance
Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)

a.title: Medication information. The model is shared by statements, requests, dispensations, and treatment lines. Each of those may have different restrictions in FHIR profile.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td:
Medication
Instances of this logical model are not marked to be the target of a Reference

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Role (active ingredient, excipient). Typically excipients are not needed, so by default active ingredients are expected.

tr.td: Concentration or presentation strength of the precise active ingredient

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck11010.png)

a.title: Type of strength that is expressed

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck110.png)

a.title: Unit of presentation for the manufactured item (tablet, vial, tube). Typically, the smallest countable object in the package.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Route of Administration - if we need it on product level

description.value: Medication information. The model is shared by statements, requests, dispensations, and treatment lines. Each of those may have different restrictions in FHIR profile.

definition.value: Medication information. The model is shared by statements, requests, dispensations, and treatment lines. Each of those may have different restrictions in FHIR profile.

short.value: MPID or national identifier

definition.value: MPID or national identifier

short.value: PCID or national

definition.value: PCID or national

short.value: PhPID or national virtual/administrable product code

definition.value: PhPID or national virtual/administrable product code

short.value: Medication code from a terminology

definition.value: Medication code from a terminology

short.value: ATC code

definition.value: ATC code

short.value: Classification (narcotic/psychotropic; orphan drug; etc)

definition.value: Classification (narcotic/psychotropic; orphan drug; etc)

short.value: Overall amount of product in one virtual or real package (100ml; 20 tablets; 1 creme & 6 pessaries)

definition.value: Overall amount of product in one virtual or real package (100ml; 20 tablets; 1 creme & 6 pessaries)

short.value: Name of the product (full name, invented name, other)

definition.value: Name of the product (full name, invented name, other)

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Name of type and language that is relevant for the users

definition.value: Name of type and language that is relevant for the users

short.value: Language of the name

definition.value: Language of the name

short.value: Type of the name (full name, common name, etc)

definition.value: Type of the name (full name, common name, etc)

short.value: Dose form(s) on a product level. Dose form for a single package item is defined below.

definition.value: Dose form(s) on a product level. Dose form for a single package item is defined below.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Coded dose form

definition.value: Coded dose form

short.value: The type of dose form (combined, authorised, administrable...)

definition.value: The type of dose form (combined, authorised, administrable...)

short.value: A medication item. For combination packs, this can be manufactured items with each item having its own dose form and ingredients+strengths defined

definition.value: A medication item. For combination packs, this can be manufactured items with each item having its own dose form and ingredients+strengths defined

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

short.value: Role (active ingredient, excipient). Typically excipients are not needed, so by default active ingredients are expected.

definition.value: Concentration or presentation strength

short.value: Manufactured item quantity for liquids (3ml/vial)

definition.value: Manufactured item quantity for liquids (3ml/vial)

short.value: Number of such manufactured items in this product (5 vials)

definition.value: Number of such manufactured items in this product (5 vials)

short.value: Administrative device included in the product

definition.value: Administrative device included in the product

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Extensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized

definition.value:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.
Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

requirements.value: Modifier extensions allow for extensions that *cannot* be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the [definition of modifier extensions](http://hl7.org/fhir/R5/extensibility.html#modifierExtension).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

isModifierReason.value: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

short.value: Number of such devices

definition.value: Number of such devices

short.value: Device coded

definition.value: Device coded

short.value: MAH name - do we need more?

definition.value: MAH name - do we need more?

short.value: Route of Administration - if we need it on product level

definition.value: Route of Administration - if we need it on product level
Medication information. The model is shared by statements, requests, dispensations, and treatment lines. Each of those may have different restrictions in FHIR profile.

short.value: MPID or national identifier

definition.value: MPID or national identifier

short.value: PCID or national

definition.value: PCID or national

short.value: PhPID or national virtual/administrable product code

definition.value: PhPID or national virtual/administrable product code

short.value: Medication code from a terminology

definition.value: Medication code from a terminology

short.value: ATC code

definition.value: ATC code

short.value: Classification (narcotic/psychotropic; orphan drug; etc)

definition.value: Classification (narcotic/psychotropic; orphan drug; etc)

short.value: Overall amount of product in one virtual or real package (100ml; 20 tablets; 1 creme & 6 pessaries)

definition.value: Overall amount of product in one virtual or real package (100ml; 20 tablets; 1 creme & 6 pessaries)

short.value: Name of the product (full name, invented name, other)

definition.value: Name of the product (full name, invented name, other)

short.value: Name of type and language that is relevant for the users

definition.value: Name of type and language that is relevant for the users

short.value: Language of the name

definition.value: Language of the name

short.value: Type of the name (full name, common name, etc)

definition.value: Type of the name (full name, common name, etc)

short.value: Dose form(s) on a product level. Dose form for a single package item is defined below.

definition.value: Dose form(s) on a product level. Dose form for a single package item is defined below.

short.value: Coded dose form

definition.value: Coded dose form

short.value: The type of dose form (combined, authorised, administrable...)

definition.value: The type of dose form (combined, authorised, administrable...)

short.value: A medication item. For combination packs, this can be manufactured items with each item having its own dose form and ingredients+strengths defined

definition.value: A medication item. For combination packs, this can be manufactured items with each item having its own dose form and ingredients+strengths defined

short.value: Dose form of single item

definition.value: Dose form of single item

short.value: Dose form

definition.value: Dose form

short.value: The type of dose form (typically manufactured dose form)

definition.value: The type of dose form (typically manufactured dose form)

short.value: Role (active ingredient, excipient). Typically excipients are not needed, so by default active ingredients are expected.

definition.value: Role (active ingredient, excipient). Typically excipients are not needed, so by default active ingredients are expected.

short.value: Concentration or presentation strength

definition.value: Concentration or presentation strength

short.value: Concentration or presentation strength of the precise active ingredient

definition.value: Concentration or presentation strength of the precise active ingredient

short.value: Type of strength that is expressed

definition.value: Type of strength that is expressed

short.value: Substance that the strength refers to, if different from the main substance

definition.value: Substance that the strength refers to, if different from the main substance

short.value: Unit of presentation for the manufactured item (tablet, vial, tube). Typically, the smallest countable object in the package.

definition.value: Unit of presentation for the manufactured item (tablet, vial, tube). Typically, the smallest countable object in the package.

short.value: Manufactured item quantity for liquids (3ml/vial)

definition.value: Manufactured item quantity for liquids (3ml/vial)

short.value: Number of such manufactured items in this product (5 vials)

definition.value: Number of such manufactured items in this product (5 vials)

short.value: Administrative device included in the product

definition.value: Administrative device included in the product

short.value: Number of such devices

definition.value: Number of such devices

short.value: Device coded

definition.value: Device coded

short.value: MAH name - do we need more?

definition.value: MAH name - do we need more?

short.value: Route of Administration - if we need it on product level

definition.value: Route of Administration - if we need it on product level
